name: Korey Stringer
title: Last known photo of Korey Stringer, taken at Minnesota Vikings training camp on July 31, 2001 — he died the following day from heatstroke.
description: Korey Stringer, an offensive tackle for the Minnesota Vikings, was photographed at the team's training camp in Mankato, Minnesota, on July 31, 2001. During practice in extreme heat — temperatures reached 99°F with high humidity — Stringer vomited and had to leave the field. He returned to practice the next morning on August 1, but collapsed from exertional heatstroke. His body temperature was recorded at 108.8°F. He was rushed to Immanuel St. Joseph's Hospital, where he died that afternoon from organ failure caused by heatstroke. He was 27 years old. His death led to sweeping changes in NFL training camp heat safety protocols.
bio: Korey Stringer was an American professional football player who played offensive tackle for the Minnesota Vikings. His death from heatstroke during training camp led to significant changes in NFL heat safety protocols.
